subject: Economics
q: Comparison of interpersonal utility is impossible because
explanation: The comparison of interpersonal utility is impossible because utility is subjectively determined. This means that utility, or the satisfaction that a person gets from consuming a good or service, is based on individual preferences and personal circumstances. Therefore, it is not possible to compare the utility of one person with that of another.

topics.title: THEORY OF CONSUMER BEHAVIOUR : UTILITY SCHOOL OF THOUGHT

subject: Economics
q: When the shape of the total utility curve is declining, the marginal utility of a consumer will be increasing if he
explanation:
Marginal utility refers to the additional satisfaction a consumer derives from the consumption of an additional unit of a product. As more and more of a product is consumed by that consumer, the utility/satisfaction he gets reduces.
Therefore, the marginal utility of a product is greater when a person has fewer of them, but as the accumulation of goods increases, the marginal utility of additional goods declines.

topics.title: THEORY OF CONSUMER BEHAVIOUR : DIMINISHING MARGINAL UTILITY AND LAW OF DEMAND

topics.title: THEORY OF CONSUMER BEHAVIOUR : CONSUMER EQUILIBRIUM

subject: Economics
q: The supply of cocoa is influenced by
explanation: The supply of a commodity like cocoa is influenced by various factors. One of the key factors is seasonal conditions. Cocoa is a crop that depends on specific weather conditions for its growth. Therefore, changes in weather patterns or seasonal conditions can significantly affect the supply of cocoa. The efficacy of fertilizer used, the demand for beverages, and the availability of close substitutes can also influence the supply of cocoa, but they are not as direct or significant as seasonal conditions.

subject: Economics
q: If quantity is constant irrespective of price changes, the supply elasticity is
explanation: Elasticity of supply measures the responsiveness of quantity supplied to a change in price. If the quantity supplied remains constant irrespective of price changes, it means the supply is perfectly inelastic. In this case, suppliers are not responsive to price changes.

topics.title: THEORY OF SUPPLY : ELASTICITY OF SUPPLY

subject: Economics
q: The cost elasticity of supply is a useful instrument for measuring
explanation: The price/cost elasticity of supply measures the degree to which a change in price would affect the quantity of goods the producer is willing to produce and supply.

topics.title: THE CONCEPT OF COST & REVENUE : THE MARGINAL COST & SUPPLY CURVE OF A FIRM

subject: Economics
q: The invisible hand promotes the interests of
explanation: According to Adam Smith, the working of the invisible hand is to the benefit of the society. Investing in an industry with the aim of making a profit represents an "invisible" hand where the interests of the society are promoted while enriching the person who has made the investment.

topics.title: INTRODUCTION TO ECONOMICS : BASIC CONCEPT OF ECONOMICS

subject: Economics
q: Fixing price above equilibrium will cause
explanation: Fixing prices above equilibrium will cause an increase in supply because suppliers will be willing to produce more since they will sell at a high price

topics.title: THE THEORY OF PRICE DETERMINATION : DETERMINANTS OF PRICE UNDER FREE MARKET

subject: Economics
q: An important function of the price system is to
explanation: Allocate resources to most productive uses. The price signals producers on the quantity to produce. Hence using available resources to produce the quantity of goods in demand.

topics.title: THE THEORY OF PRICE DETERMINATION : CONCEPT OF MARKET AND PRICE

subject: Economics
q: If all factors are variable in the long run, firm will experience
explanation: In the long run, all factors of production are variable. This means that a firm can adjust all its inputs in response to changes in market conditions. This flexibility allows the firm to achieve economies of scale, which refers to the cost advantages that firms obtain due to size, output, or scale of operation, with cost per unit of output generally decreasing with increasing scale as fixed costs are spread out over more units of output. Therefore, the correct answer is 'economies of scale'.

topics.title: THEORY OF PRODUCTION (2) : PRODUCTION FUNCTIONS & LAWS OF RETURNS

subject: Economics
q: The equilibrium point of a firm is attained at the point where the isoquant is
explanation: The firm is in equilibrium at the point where the isoquant curve is tangent to the iso-cost line. At this point, the firm is maximising its output level by employing the optimal combination of capital and labour.

subject: Economics
q: The long-run average cost curve touches the short-run average cost curve at the
options: minimum points of all short-run average cost curves
declining points of all short-run average cost curves
minimum point of only one of the short-run curves
rising points of all short-run average cost curves.
explanation:
The Long-Run Average Cost (LRAC) curve is derived from a series of Short-Run Average Cost (SRAC) curves. It is often called the envelope curve because it envelopes or touches each SRAC curve at exactly one point — specifically at the minimum point of the SRAC where the firm is optimally using plant size or scale for that level of output.

At this point, the firm's plant size (fixed inputs) is perfectly suited to the level of production, so the short-run and long-run costs are equal.

topics.title: THE CONCEPT OF COST & REVENUE : SHORT RUN & LONG RUN COST

subject: Economics
q: If a firm doubles all inputs in the long run and the total output is less than doubled, this results in
explanation: Decreasing Returns to Scale. Decreasing returns to scale occur when a firm's output less than scales in comparison to its inputs. For example, a firm exhibits decreasing returns to scale if its output less than doubles when all of its inputs are doubled.

topics.title: THEORY OF PRODUCTION (2) : PRODUCTION FUNCTIONS & LAWS OF RETURNS

subject: Economics
q: Patents and copyrights enable monopolists to
explanation: Restricts entry of new firms. Patent rights prevents any other firm from producing exactly the same type of machine.

subject: Economics
q: A discriminatory monopoly is characterized by
explanation: Different elasticities in different markets. It exists where the same product is sold at different prices to different buyers e.g Business class & economy class in the same aircraft.

subject: Economics
q: Net National Product is derived by deducting
explanation: Net National Product (NNP) is derived by deducting depreciation from Gross National Product (GNP). Depreciation refers to the wear and tear of capital goods over a period of time. So, when we subtract the depreciation from GNP, we get NNP which represents the net output of the economy after accounting for the depreciation of capital goods.

subject: Economics
q:
Use the information below to answer the question below



The investment expenditure of an economy changes by ₦2million and MPC is 0.75.

What is the change in income?
explanation: The change in income is calculated using the multiplier effect. The formula for the multiplier is 1/(1-MPC). Given that the MPC is 0.75, the multiplier is 4. Therefore, the change in income is the change in investment expenditure multiplied by the multiplier, which is ₦2million * 4 = ₦8million.

topics.title: EQUILIBRIUM LEVEL OF NATIONAL INCOME : THE CONCEPT OF MULTIPLIER

The multiplier is
explanation: The multiplier is calculated as 1/(1-MPC). Given that the MPC is 0.75, the multiplier is 4.

subject: Economics
q: The money that commands a higher market value than its face value is called
explanation: The money that commands a higher market value than its face value is called fiat money. Fiat money is money that is not backed by any physical commodity, such as gold or silver. Instead, its value is derived from the government's decree that it must be accepted as legal tender.

subject: Economics
q: The minimum amount which banks are required to deposit with the central bank is determined by the
explanation: Cash reserve ratio (CRR)/legal reserve ratio is a certain percentage of the total deposits of the bank that must be kept in the current account with the central bank of the country, which would mean that the bank shall not have access to that amount of money for any commercial activity or economic activity.

subject: Economics
q: The major function of the money market is to
options: stabilize the value of the local currency
explanation: The money market is a component of the economy which provides short-term funds. The money market deals in short-term loans, generally for a period of a year or less.

subject: Economics
q: An ad valorem tax is imposed on
explanation: An ad valorem tax is a tax that is based on the assessed value of an item such as real estate or personal property. It is more commonly applied to duties on imported goods. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the value of a commodity'.

subject: Economics
q: A huge national debt is an indication that the gold reserves of a nation has
explanation: A large national debt does not necessarily mean that a nation has low gold reserves. However, it can put pressure on a country's gold reserves, as the country may need to sell some of its gold reserves to raise money to finance its debt.

topics.title: ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT : INDICATORS OF GROWTH & DEVELOPMENT

subject: Economics
q: Rapid economic development in Nigeria is realizable by
explanation: Economic diversification Economic diversification is the process of shifting an economy away from a single income source toward multiple sources from a growing range of sectors and markets. Traditionally, it has been applied as a strategy to encourage positive economic growth and development.

subject: Economics
q: The primary reason for desiring economic growth is to
explanation: Raise standard of living. Standard of living is the extent to which citizens can satisfy their wants and economic growth is the increase of the total output of a country over time.

topics.title: ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT : INDICATORS OF GROWTH & DEVELOPMENT

subject: Economics
q: An emerging agriculture export crop in Nigeria is
explanation: Cassava is an emerging agriculture export crop in Nigeria. This is due to the fact that Nigeria is the world's largest producer of cassava. The government has been promoting the export of value-added cassava products like flour and starch. Therefore, the correct answer is cassava.

subject: Economics
q: A strategy for improving agriculture in Nigeria will involve
explanation: One of the ways to improve agriculture in Nigeria is too first ensure self-sufficiency. Agricultural production Nigeria should be able to cater for the needs of the people before working towards exportation. Nigeria should be able to maintain oneself or itself without outside aid.

subject: Economics
q: A sugar industry is best located near the source of
explanation: A sugar industry is best located near the source of raw materials. This is because the raw materials for sugar production, which are sugarcane or sugar beets, are bulky and perishable. Transporting them over long distances can be costly and may lead to spoilage. Therefore, to minimize transportation costs and prevent spoilage, it is best to locate the industry near the source of the raw materials.

subject: Economics
q: An important contribution of small scale industries to the Nigerian economy is in the area of
explanation: According to the Nigeria Bureau of Statistics, small and medium scale enterprises (SMEs) in Nigeria have contributed about 48% of the national GDP in the last five years. With a total number of about 17.4 million, they account for about 50% of industrial jobs and nearly 90% of the manufacturing sector, in terms of number of enterprises.

subject: Economics
q: A change in the pump price of petrol in Nigeria has a direct effect on the
explanation: A change in the pump price of petrol in Nigeria has a direct effect on the cost of transportation. This is because petrol is a major fuel used in transportation. When the price of petrol increases, the cost of transportation also increases because transporters would need to spend more on petrol. This is a basic economic principle that when the cost of an input (in this case, petrol) increases, the cost of the product or service (in this case, transportation) that uses that input also increases.

subject: Economics
q: Long-term fund of investment projects are sourced from the
explanation: Long-term funds for investment projects are sourced from the capital market. The capital market is a part of the financial market where long-term debt or equity-backed securities are bought and sold. It is where businesses and governments can raise long-term funds. It is divided into two categories, primary and secondary markets. The primary market is where new securities are sold for the first time, while the secondary market is where existing securities are traded.

topics.title: FINANCIAL INSTITUTION : DEVELOPMENT, MERCHANT, MORTGAGE BANK ETC

subject: Economics
q: A policy aimed at enhancing globalization of the Nigerian economy is
explanation: Deregulation is a policy aimed at enhancing globalization of the Nigerian economy. Deregulation is the process of removing or reducing state regulations, typically in the economic sphere. It is the repeal of governmental regulation of the economy. It became common in advanced industrial economies in the 1970s and 1980s, as a result of new trends in economic thinking about the inefficiencies of government regulation, and the risk that regulatory agencies would be controlled by the regulated industry to its benefit, and thereby hurt consumers and the wider economy.

subject: Economics
q: The Malthusian Theory was concerned about the relationship between
options: population growth rates of the rich and the poor nations
explanation: The Malthusian theory of population examines the relationship between population growth and food supply. It states that the population grows exponentially, while its food supply only grows at a linear rate. That eventually causes the population to outgrow its food supply, which leads to a Malthusian crisis.

subject: Economics
q: In a village of 50 persons, 10 immigrated, 25 died and 5 emigrated in a year. Determine the total population.
explanation:
Total population = Birth rate - Death rate + Net migration
Where net migration = Immigrant - Emigrant
= 25 - ( 10-5)
= 25 - 5
= 20
Therefore, total population = 50 - 20
= 30

subject: Economics
q: A deficit balance is measured by subtracting the debits from the credits in the
explanation: A deficit balance is calculated by subtracting the debits from the credits. In the context of economics, this is typically done in the current account, which records a nation's transactions with the rest of the world, specifically its net trade in goods and services, its net earnings on cross-border investments, and its net transfer payments. Therefore, the correct answer is 'current account'.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL TRADE : BALANCE OF TRADE & PAYMENT

subject: Economics
q: One of the functions of ECOWAS is to
options: provide employment for citizens of member nations.
explanation: ECOWAS was also set up to promote the idea of collective self-sufficiency among the states. As a trading union, it is also meant to create a single, large trading bloc through economic cooperation among the member states. ECOWAS also functions as a peacekeeping force amongst member states.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ECONOMIC ORGANISATION : OPEC, ECA, IMF

q: The reward on machinery and equipment in the process of production is known as
explanation:
Capital' - investment in man-made aids to production including buildings, factories, computers. The reward for capital is interest.

Interest is the payment made for the use of borrowed money. In the context of production, interest is the reward paid to the owner of capital, which includes machinery and equipment.

explanation: The minimum wage tends to create an excess of supply over demand when this occurs businesses will have to lay of workers leading to unemployment.

subject: Economics
q: An economy in which both the public and private sectors contribute to economic growth is a
explanation: It is an economic system that features characteristics of both capitalism and socialism. A mixed economic system allows a level of private economic freedom in the use of capital, but also allows for governments to interfere in economic activities in order to achieve social aims.

topics.title: TOOLS FOR ECONOMIC ANALYSIS : MEASURE OF DISPERSION

subject: Economics
q: Which of the following set of statistical tools is used for further economic analysis?
explanation: In economic analysis, the mean and standard deviation are often used together. The mean provides a measure of central tendency, giving an average value for the data set, while the standard deviation provides a measure of dispersion, showing how spread out the data is from the mean. Therefore, the correct option is 'The mean and standard deviation'.

topics.title: TOOLS FOR ECONOMIC ANALYSIS : MEASURE OF CENTRAL TENDENCY

subject: Economics
q: An advantage of the range as a measure of dispersion is that, it
options: can be used to calculate open-ended distribution
makes use of all values of observations in a distribution
explanation: Range refers to the difference between each series' minimum and maximum values. The range offers us a good indication of how dispersed the data is, but we need other measures of variability to discover the dispersion of data from central tendency measurements. A range is the most common and easily understandable measure of dispersion. It is the difference between two extreme observations of the data set.

Median = 22

an increase in equilibrium price and quantity
a decrease in equilibrium price and quantity
an increase in equilibrium price and a decrease in equilibrium quantity
explanation: When the demand increases while supply remains constant, there will be an excess demand over supply, also there will be an increase in equilibrium price and quantity.

explanation: An increase in price of a commodity will generally lead to a decrease in the quantity of the commodity demanded per time period.

subject: Economics
q: One of the assumptions of the cardinalist approach is
options: that total utility depends on the quantity of the commodities consumed
explanation: The cardinalist approach asserts that utility can be measured and quantified. It means, it is possible to express utility that an individual derives from consuming a commodity in quantitative terms. Thus, a person may express the utility he derives from consuming an apple as 10 utils or 20 utils. Moreover, it allows consumers to compare and define the difference in utilities perceived in two commodites. Thus, it allows an individual to state that commodity A (accruing a utility of 20 utils) gives double the utility of commodity B ( which accrues a utility of 10 utils).

topics.title: THEORY OF CONSUMER BEHAVIOUR : UTILITY SCHOOL OF THOUGHT

subject: Economics
q: Utility is the satisfaction derived from the
explanation: Utility is the satisfaction derived from the consumption of a particular commodity. Utility is a measure of preferences over some set of goods and services.

explanation: The Consumer will prefer point S since point S has a higher indifference curve and Utility is higher than the other points on the graph.

subject: Economics
q: One of the major factors that brings about changes in supply is
explanation:
The available of facilities to store produced goods would affect the quantity of goods supplied to the market. Producers maybe willing to produce, but if there are no storage facilities to store the manufactured goods pending when the demand for it will arise, producers would cut down on production thereby affecting the quantity of goods supplied.
Others factors are:
(i) Natural Conditions (ii) Technical Progress (iii) Change in Factor Prices (iv) Transport Improvements (v) Calamities (vi) Monopolies (vii) Fiscal Policy.

subject: Economics
q: If the supply of a product is elastic, a small reduction in price will
options: lead to no change in the quantity supplied
explanation: If supply is elastic, an increase in demand will cause only a small rise in price, but a significant increase in demand. If supply is inelastic, an increase in demand will cause a large rise in price but only a small increase in demand.

topics.title: THEORY OF SUPPLY : ELASTICITY OF SUPPLY

subject: Economics
q: The supply of beverages by firms in a monopolistic market is an example of
explanation:
This is a more realistic scenario that actually occurs in the real world. In monopolistic competition, there are still a large number of buyers as well as sellers. But they all do not sell homogeneous products. The products are similar but all sellers sell slightly differentiated products.

Now the consumers have the preference of choosing one product over another. The sellers can also charge a marginally higher price since they may enjoy some market power. So the sellers become the price setters to a certain extent.

For example, the market for cereals is a monopolistic competition. The products are all similar but slightly differentiated in terms of taste and flavours. Another such example is toothpaste.

subject: Economics
q: If the price of a commodity is fixed below equilibrium, this will lead to
explanation: If the of a commodity is fixed below the equilibrium price, there will be excess demand over supply which will lead to shortage.

topics.title: THE THEORY OF PRICE DETERMINATION : DETERMINANTS OF PRICE UNDER FREE MARKET

subject: Economics
q: One of the criticisms of the price mechanism is that
explanation: The price mechanism, which is the economic concept that the free market system is sufficient to determine the price of goods and services through supply and demand, is often criticized for widening the inequitable gap. This is because it can lead to wealth disparities, where those who can afford to pay more for goods and services get more, while those who cannot afford to pay as much get less.

topics.title: THE THEORY OF PRICE DETERMINATION : PRICE LEGISLATION

subject: Economics
q: In Nigeria, government can reduce the cost of accommodation by fixing rent
explanation: The government can reduce the cost of accommodation by fixing the rent at a maximum price. Maximum prices occur when a government sets a legal limit on the price of a good or service – with the aim of reducing prices below the market equilibrium price. Governments try to control the rate charged for rent by keeping the cost of renting below a certain level. However, setting the price below equilibrium maximum will cause a shortage – demand will be greater than supply.

subject: Economics
q: If a refinery achieves a reduction in cost by purchasing and transporting crude in large quantities, it enjoys
explanation: Economies of scale are the cost advantages that enterprises obtain due to size, output, or scale of operation, with cost per unit of output generally decreasing with increasing scale as fixed costs are spread out over more units of output.

topics.title: THEORY OF PRODUCTION (2) : CONCEPTS OF PRODUCTION

subject: Economics
q: An isoquant lying above to the right of another represents
explanation: An isoquant is also called as-equal product curve or production indifference curve or constant product curve. An isoquant lying above to the right of another will represent a higher output level.

topics.title: THEORY OF PRODUCTION (2) : LAWS OF PRODUCTION

subject: Economics
q: From the graph above, R represents
explanation:
R represents Total cost which is the total economic cost of production and is made up of variable cost and fixed cost.
M is variable cost while T is fixed cost.

topics.title: THE CONCEPT OF COST & REVENUE : THE MARGINAL COST & SUPPLY CURVE OF A FIRM

subject: Economics
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question


The optimum level output of the firm is
explanation: The Point OB is the optimal level of output which indicates the point of intersection of MR and MC.

topics.title: THE CONCEPT OF COST & REVENUE : PROFIT MAXIMIZATION

subject: Economics
q:
Use the diagram below to answer this question

The total revenue of the firm is represented by
explanation: The total revenue of the firm is point GHOb (GH representing the price and OB representing quantity). Hence, TR = GH × OB

topics.title: THE CONCEPT OF COST & REVENUE : THE CONCEPT OF REVENUE

subject: Economics
q: A measure of national income used as a comparison of standard of living among nations is
explanation: Per capita income (PCI) or average income measures the average income earned per person in a given area (city, region, country, etc.) in a specified year. It is calculated by dividing the area's total income by its total population. Per capita income is national income divided by population size. Per capita is used to measure the standard of living between two countries.

topics.title: NATIONAL INCOME : USES & REASONS FOR MEASURING NATIONAL INCOME

subject: Economics
q: The speculative demand for money is inversely related to the
explanation: The speculative motive relates to the desire to hold one's resources in liquid form to take advantage of future changes in the rate of interest or bond prices. It is inversely related with the Bond prices and the rate of interest.

subject: Economics
q: If Mr K obtains a ₦50,000 loan from a bank for the purpose of providing household needs, the demand for money is said to be
explanation: The demand for money is said to be transactionary when it is used for day-to-day transactions such as buying goods and services. In this case, Mr K is obtaining a loan to provide for household needs, which falls under day-to-day transactions. Therefore, the demand for money is transactionary. Speculative demand for money is when people hold money to speculate on future changes in interest rates or prices.

subject: Economics
q: Which of the following is used by the Central Bank of Nigeria to control inflation?
explanation: Discount rate is the minimum interest rate set by the central bank of a country. It is used in controlling inflation.

subject: Economics
q: If CBN reduces money supply, the interest rate will
explanation: The interest rates will be high if CBN reduces money supply. This is done to discourage borrowing and control the flow of money in the economy. It is mostly done when there is excess money in circulation.

subject: Economics
q: An example of an expansionary fiscal policy action is
options: decrease in the corporate profit tax rates
explanation: Lowering taxes and raising government spending. Social security measures taken by the govt. is an example of expansionary policy. Subsidies, Tax rate cuts etc are other examples There is a few example of expansionary fiscal policy.

subject: Economics
q: A tax on land will ultimately fall
explanation: A land value tax is a progressive tax, in that the tax burden falls on titleholders in proportion to the value of locations, the ownership of which is highly correlated with overall wealth and income.

subject: Economics
q: One of the goals of development plans in Nigeria is to
options: achieve higher standard of living for the citizens
explanation: The Sustainable Development Goals are a global call to action to end poverty, protect the earth's environment and climate, and ensure that people everywhere can enjoy peace and prosperity. These are the goals the UN is working on in Nigeria. In terms of SDG driven development, goal eight lends itself in particular to the Nigerian reality: "to promote sustained, inclusive and sustainable economic growth, full and productive employment and decent work for all".

subject: Economics
q: Life insurance companies contribute to economic development by holding a part of their assets in
explanation: The life insurance sector may contribute to economic growth by its very mechanism of savings mobilisation and thereby performing an intermediation role in the economy. This ensures that capital is provided to deficient units who are in need of capital to finance their working capital requirements and invest in technology thereby resulting in an increase in output. Life insurance is an institution in capital market.

topics.title: FINANCIAL INSTITUTION : DEVELOPMENT, MERCHANT, MORTGAGE BANK ETC

subject: Economics
q: In order to add value to Nigeria agricultural produce, there is need to
explanation: Most international consumers would rather have the agricultural produce processed and packaged in a way that facilitates their delivery and entry into the export market. Changing a raw agricultural product into something new through processing, cooling, drying, extracting, packaging or any other type of value addition that differentiates the final product from the original raw commodity has a value chain reaction that not only yields revenue but also creates jobs and boosts the Gross Domestic Product (GDP).

subject: Economics
q: The main reason for low agricultural produce in West Africa is
options: the use of crude implements in farming process
explanation:
The problems of agricultural development in West Africa are:
- land tenure system
- inadquate finance or credit facilities
- poor transportation systems
- inadequate storage and processing
- inadequate farm input
- illiteracy of the farmers
- lack of basic amenities
- use of crude implements
- lack of technical know- how etc

subject: Economics
q: The most important determinant for the location of a brick industry is the availability of
explanation: The most important determinant for the location of a brick industry is the availability of raw materials. This is because the production process in a brick industry heavily relies on raw materials like clay, sand etc. If these materials are not readily available, the cost of production would increase significantly due to transportation costs. Therefore, to minimize costs, brick industries are usually located near the source of raw materials.

subject: Economics
q: In Nigeria, efficiency in public corporations can be achieved through
explanation: Privatization is the transfer of ownership of property or businesses from a government to a privately owned entity.

subject: Economics
q: Government participation in the oil industry was necessitated by the
explanation: Government participation in the oil industry was necessitated by the huge investment outlay. The oil industry requires a large amount of capital for exploration, drilling, refining, and distribution. This huge investment outlay often necessitates government participation to ensure that the industry is properly funded and managed.

topics.title: PETROLEUM AND THE NIGERIA ECONOMY : INTRODUCTION

subject: Economics
q: The deregulation of the petroleum sector in Nigeria will bring about
options: efficiency in pricing and distribution of the products
an end to the importation of fuel
explanation: In this context it is the removal of regulations and restrictions such as subsidy paid by the Nigerian government on petroleum products. One of the main reasons for deregulating the petroleum sector is to make the sector competitive, efficient and with lower product prices.

topics.title: PETROLEUM AND THE NIGERIA ECONOMY : INTRODUCTION

subject: Economics
q: A distinguishing characteristic of consumer co-operative society is that the
explanation: Some of the main characteristics of consumers co-operative stores are as follows: These are known as consumer co-operative stores. These are owned and managed by the consumers themselves in order to eliminate middlemen and to obtain their requirements at reasonable prices. They are also termed as consumer co­operative societies.

subject: Economics
q: A major disadvantage of partnership business is
explanation: A major disadvantage of partnership business is the difficulty in the transfer of shares. In a partnership, shares cannot be transferred to a new partner without the consent of all existing partners. This can make it difficult for partners to exit the business or for new partners to join.

subject: Economics
q: The quality of labour force in Nigeria can be improved by
options: encouraging the study of science and technology
explanation: The quality of the labour force depends on education and training, physique, and health. There is evidence that physique has been greatly improved by increases in the standard of living in the 20th century.

topics.title: LABOUR MARKET AND UNEMPLOYMENT : SUPPLY OF LABOUR

subject: Economics
q: The effect of emigration on a country's population is
explanation: The effect on the emigrating country is a reduction in the population and labour force and an increase in the population of the new country of residence.

subject: Economics
q: A measure for preventing the external value of the Naira from falling is for the government to
options: buy its own currency with foreign reserve
explanation: The bank can buy its own currency by using foreign currency that it has in its own reserves. This not only cuts off the currency's depreciation, but also controls the money supply by reducing the amount in circulation. The same is true if the central bank decides to do the opposite—by selling its own currency if it appreciates too much.

subject: Economics
q: A fiscal policy instrument that can influence the demand pattern in an economy is
explanation: Fiscal policy refers to the use of government revenue collection (taxation) and expenditure (spending) to influence a country's economy. The primary instruments of fiscal policy are government spending and taxation. Therefore, government spending is a fiscal policy instrument that can influence the demand pattern in an economy.

subject: Economics
q: One of the main achievements of the Economic Commission for Africa is
options: guaranteeing a steady flow of foreign investment into Africa
providing the machinery for collaboration on monetary issues
explanation: The Economic Commission for Africa (ECA) is a regional commission of the United Nations that aims to promote the economic and social development of its member states. One of its main achievements is encouraging transport and communication development among African states. This is because the ECA has been instrumental in promoting infrastructure development, including transport and communication, to facilitate trade and economic growth in the region.

subject: Economics
q: The rate of interest charged on loans depends largely on
explanation: The rate of interest charged on loans is largely dependent on the risk associated with the loan. This is because the higher the risk of the loan, the higher the interest rate charged to compensate for the potential risk of default. The prevailing exchange rate, marginal efficiency of capital, and the prevailing tax rate may affect the interest rate, but they are not the primary factors.

subject: Economics
q: A valid explanation for real wage growth is
options: an increase in the rate of productivity
a contraction of employment in service industries
an increase in the quantity of labour
explanation: Real productivity increase was thus more than four times the increase in real wages, which means that three quarter of the achieved labour productivity growth was not paid out in form of wages. In Hungary, Romania, Portugal and Greece real wages went down in the last 16 years, while productivity increased.

topics.title: FACTORS OF PRODUCTION & THEIR THEORIES : WAGES

subject: Economics
q: If Mr X lost his clerical job at a store and searched for a similar job for ten months before finding one, this implies that Mr X was
explanation: Frictional unemployment is a type of unemployment. It is sometimes called search unemployment and can be based on the circumstances of the individual. It is time spent between jobs when a worker is searching for a job or transitioning from one job to another

topics.title: LABOUR MARKET AND UNEMPLOYMENT : THE CONCEPT OF UNEMPLOYMENT

subject: Economics
q: What subject is this?
explanation: The subject is indicated in the question is Economics.

topics.title: INTRODUCTION TO ECONOMICS : BASIC CONCEPT OF ECONOMICS

subject: Economics
q: The choice of how to produce in a command economy is determined by
explanation: A command economy is a system where the government, rather than the free market, determines what goods should be produced, how much should be produced, and the price at which the goods are offered for sale. It also determines investments and incomes. The command economy is a key feature of any communist society.

topics.title: ECONOMIC SYSTEM : ECONOMIC PROBLEMS OF THE SOCIETY

subject: Economics
q: In capitalist economies, questions about what to produce are ultimately answered by
explanation: Capitalism is an economic system in which private individuals or businesses own capital goods. The production of goods and services is based on supply and demand in the general market—known as a market economy—rather than through central planning—known as a planned economy or command economy.

topics.title: ECONOMIC SYSTEM : ECONOMIC PROBLEMS OF THE SOCIETY

subject: Economics
q: The best measure of dispersion to determine the tallest tree in a forest is
explanation: The range is the best measure of dispersion to determine the tallest tree in a forest. The range is the difference between the highest and lowest values in a set of data. In this case, it would be the difference between the height of the tallest and shortest tree. The other options, variance, standard deviation, and mean deviation, are measures of dispersion that provide information about the spread of data around the mean, not the extreme values.

topics.title: TOOLS FOR ECONOMIC ANALYSIS : MEASURE OF DISPERSION

explanation:
Let x represent services

Services = 360-(x + 72 + 108 + 126)= 54°

Percentage contribution = 54/360 x 100
= 15%

subject: Economics
q:
Use the pie chart below to answer the question that follows





If the national income is ₦60m, the contribution of the manufacturing sector is
explanation:
Manufacturing contribution = 108/360 x ₦60m
= ₦18m

subject: Economics
q: A combination of factors responsible for a shift from D0D0 to D1D1 are
options: Price of the commodity and weather conditions
explanation: The points from DoDo to D1D1 implies increase in demand and this could be caused by increase in consumer's income or population size, effective advertising etc.

topics.title: THE THEORY OF DEMAND : MEANING AND DETERMINANTS

explanation:

16 = 8P
P = ₦2

topics.title: THE THEORY OF PRICE DETERMINATION : DETERMINANTS OF PRICE UNDER FREE MARKET

subject: Economics
q: A change in demand for a normal good implies that, there is a
options: Change in the price elasticity of demand
Change in the quantity demanded as price change
explanation: A normal good is a good that experiences an increase in its demand due to a rise in consumers' income. In other words, if there's an increase in wages, demand for normal goods increases while conversely, wage declines or layoffs lead to a reduction in demand.

topics.title: THE THEORY OF DEMAND : DISTINCTION BETWEEN CHANGE IN QUANTITY DEMANDED AND CHANGE IN DEMAND

subject: Economics
q: If a 10% rise in price causes a 5% decrease in the quantity demanded of a commodity, the elasticity of demand is
explanation:
e = % change in quantity demanded/ % change in price
e = 5/10
e = 0.5. It is inelastic because an increase in price leads to fall in the quantity demanded

q: From the graph above, the consumer will attain equilibrium at point
explanation: The consumer attains equilibrium at point K which is higher than J and M. This is the point of optimal satisfaction for the consumer where the indifference curve is tangential to the budget line.

subject: Economics
q: A rightward shift of the budget line is caused by a
explanation: The fall in the commodity's price increase the consumer's purchasing power which is represented by a rightward shift in the budget line

subject: Economics
q: When price is set below equilibrium this will lead to
explanation: When the price is set below the equilibrium,there will be excess demand over supply which will lead to shortage.

subject: Economics
q: Price mechanism determines the price of commodities through
explanation: The price mechanism is an economic concept that refers to the way the supply and demand of a commodity or service interact to determine its price. This is done through market forces, which include the behaviors of buyers and sellers. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Market forces'.

topics.title: THEORY OF PRODUCTION (2) : PRODUCTION FUNCTIONS & LAWS OF RETURNS

subject: Economics
q: If the production cost of a large firm is higher than that of a small firm, it is experiencing
explanation: If the production cost of a large firm is higher than that of a small firm, it is experiencing internal diseconomies of scale. Diseconomies of scale occur when a business grows so large that the costs per unit increase. It takes place when economies of scale no longer function for a firm. With this increase in output, the cost per unit output increases. It is the result of inefficiency in the firm.

topics.title: THEORY OF PRODUCTION (1) : SCALE OF PRODUCTION

subject: Economics
q: Division of labour requires that, the tasks in a production line be performed
explanation: Division of labor can be defined simply as the splitting or separation of a particular work process into various processes such that each process is undertaken by a different worker or different group of workers. Division of labour is done in stages.

topics.title: THEORY OF PRODUCTION (1) : DIVISION OF LABOUR & SPECIALISATION

topics.title: THE CONCEPT OF COST & REVENUE : SHORT RUN & LONG RUN COST

subject: Economics
q: Rent and administrative expenses are examples of
explanation: Rent and administrative expenses are examples of fixed costs. Fixed costs are costs that do not change with the level of output. They are incurred even when the firm is not producing anything. They include costs like rent, salaries, and administrative expenses.

topics.title: THE CONCEPT OF COST & REVENUE : EXPLICIT AND IMPLICIT COSTS

subject: Economics
q: A perfect competitor will continue to expand output up to the point where
explanation: A perfect competitor will continue to expand output up to the point where Marginal Revenue (MR) equals Average Revenue (AR). This is because in perfect competition, the price is given and constant, hence, the average revenue, which is also the price, equals the marginal revenue.

subject: Economics
q: One of the characteristics of a monopolist is that, he can influence
explanation: The monopolist can either influence price or quantity but it cannot influence both. Before a monopolist will charge or influence his price, he has to look at his elasticity.

subject: Economics
q: Monopolist can boost his revenue by
options: Reducing total output to march the price
explanation:
A monopolist is a firm that has no close competitors and faces a downward-sloping demand curve. This means that the monopolist can set the price of its output, and the quantity demanded will decrease as the price increases.

If a monopolist increases the price of its output, its total revenue will increase, even if the quantity demanded decreases. This is because the increase in price will outweigh the decrease in quantity demanded.

subject: Economics
q: Which of the following can be used to measure the Gross National Product in an open economy?
explanation: For an open economy, GNP is calculated as: C+I+G+(X-M) where C = consumption expenditure, I= investment expenditure G = government expenditures X = exports and M =imports.

topics.title: NATIONAL INCOME : MEASUREMENT OF NATIONAL INCOME

subject: Economics
q: If MPC = 2/3 and investment is ₦100 million. The level of national income is
explanation:
MPC = 2/3

Since K = 1/1 - MPC
Then, K = 1 / 1 - 2/3
= 3

Y = K x Δ I = 3 x 100
= ₦300m

topics.title: EQUILIBRIUM LEVEL OF NATIONAL INCOME : THE CONCEPT OF MULTIPLIER

subject: Economics
q: The precautionary demand for money is determined by
explanation: The precautionary demand for money depends upon the level of income ( i.e direct) , and business activity, opportunities for unexpected profitable deals, availability of cash, the cost of holding liquid assets in bank reserves, etc. Keynes held that the precautionary demand for money, like transactions demand, was a function of the level of income.

subject: Economics
q: An inflation that co-exists with high rate of unemployment is
explanation: This is a situation when an economy is facing the twin macroeconomic problems of inflation and unemployment or inflation with stagflation.

subject: Economics
q: One of the challenges facing the banking industry in Nigeria is
explanation: One of the major challenges facing the banking industry in Nigeria is ensuring technological security. With the advent of digital banking, ensuring the security of customers' data and transactions has become a major concern. This includes protecting against cyber attacks, fraud, and other forms of financial crime.

subject: Economics
q: Short-terms loans for investment are usually obtained through the
explanation: The money market deals with short term loans in contrary to the capital market which deals with medium and long term loans.

topics.title: FINANCIAL INSTITUTION : DEVELOPMENT, MERCHANT, MORTGAGE BANK ETC

subject: Economics
q: Given a base year and price index of 175% the following year. Which of the following will arise
options: The cost of living decreases in that year
explanation: This implies that the cost of living (i.e price) has risen by 75% and consequently, the value of money will also fall by the same 75%. The value of money and price index is inversely related.

subject: Economics
q: Wage freeze is a policy measure aimed at
explanation: Wage freeze is a policy measure that is often used to curb inflation. Inflation is a general increase in prices and fall in the purchasing value of money. When wages are frozen, it means that there will be no increase in salaries for a period of time. This can help to slow down the rate of inflation because when wages increase, people have more money to spend, which can drive up prices. Therefore, by freezing wages, the government can help to control the rate of inflation.

subject: Economics
q: A major obstacle for the development of Nigerian economy is
explanation: Over dependence on oil is a major obstacle for the development of the Nigerian economy. This is because the Nigerian economy is heavily reliant on oil exports for its revenue. When oil prices are high, the economy does well. However, when oil prices fall, the economy suffers. This over reliance on oil makes the economy vulnerable to fluctuations in oil prices. Diversifying the economy and reducing dependence on oil would help to make the economy more resilient.

subject: Economics
q: A major feature of underdeveloped economy is
explanation:
The features or characteristics of an underdeveloped economy or country is:
- dependence on agriculture
- high dependency on foreign nations
- low savings and investment
- population explosion
- low per capita income
- low standard of living
- high deatn rate
- high level of illiteracy
- high level of unemployment
- low rate of economic growth

topics.title: ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT : INDICATORS OF GROWTH & DEVELOPMENT

subject: Economics
q: An important role of Agriculture in Nigeria's economy development is the
explanation: It helps to maintain a healthy and peaceful population and also a source of food and nutrition for households. In Nigeria, agriculture exports have played an important role in economic development by providing the needed foreign exchange earnings and also It helps to generate employment opportunities for the unemployed youths.

subject: Economics
q: An advantage of large-scale farming over peasant farming is in the area of
options: Redistribution national income to various regions of the country
Providing research and massive employment of labour
explanation: The benefit of large scale farming over peasant farming is the reduced cost of food. Consolidation and vertical coordination have not only reduced costs, but also expanded diversity of food products and solved information problems. Due to its extensive operations, it encourages research and allows massive employment of labour.

subject: Economics
q: A major disadvantage of localization of industry is
explanation: Localization of industry often leads to a concentration of similar businesses in a specific area. While this can create a strong local economy and specialization, it also makes the area more vulnerable to economic shifts and changes in demand for the specific industry. If the localized industry declines or becomes obsolete, it can result in structural unemployment, where workers' skills are no longer in demand and they may find it difficult to find new employment in the same area without retraining.

subject: Economics
q: One major factor that determined the location of industry is
explanation: Proximity to market is a major factor that determines industry location. Others includes: proximity to source of raw materials, availability of capital, nearness to source of power, availability of labour, adequate transport network, political stability, favourable climate etc.

subject: Economics
q: The major contribution of OPEC to the Nigeria economy is the
explanation: OPEC aims to regulate the supply of oil in order to set the price on the world market. Stabilization of oil price is a major contribution of OPEC to the Nigerian economy.

topics.title: PETROLEUM AND THE NIGERIA ECONOMY : OPEC

subject: Economics
q: The loading of crude oil at the terminal is an activity in the
options: Upstream and downstream sector of the oil industry.
Midstream and upstream sectors of the oil industry
explanation: The loading of crude oil at the terminal is an activity in the midstream sector of the oil industry. The oil and gas industry is usually divided into three major sectors: upstream, midstream, and downstream. The upstream sector involves exploration and production, the midstream sector involves transportation, storage, and wholesale marketing, while the downstream sector involves refining and selling. Therefore, the loading of crude oil at the terminal falls under the midstream sector, which involves transportation and storage.

topics.title: PETROLEUM AND THE NIGERIA ECONOMY : ONSHORE & OFFSHORE OF THE OIL SECTOR

subject: Economics
q: The short-run average variable cost of a firm will rise owing to
options: An increase in the cost of labour
An increase in the salaries of directors
explanation:
If the average cost falls due to an increase in the output, the marginal cost is less than the average cost.
If the average cost rises due to an increase in the output, the marginal cost is more than the average cost.

topics.title: THE CONCEPT OF COST & REVENUE : SHORT RUN & LONG RUN COST

subject: Economics
q: Firms embark on vertical integration in order to
options: Prevent other firm from entry the market
Take over markets formally controlled by other firms
explanation:
Vertical integration is a strategy whereby a company owns or controls its suppliers, distributors, or retail locations to control its value or supply. The following are some of the reasons for vertical integration:
Reduce transportation costs if common ownership results in closer geographic proximity.
Improve supply chain coordination.
Provide more opportunities to differentiate by means of increased control over inputs.
Capture upstream or downstream profit margins.

subject: Economics
q: The voting power in co-operative societies is vested on
explanation: In co-operative societies, the voting power is vested on the shareholders. This is because co-operative societies are owned and controlled by their members, who have equal voting rights (one member, one vote) regardless of their level of financial contribution. This is different from other types of companies where voting power is often tied to the number of shares a person holds.

subject: Economics
q: An accurate census figure is an important tool for planning in
explanation: An accurate census figure is crucial for planning in resource allocation. Census data provides detailed information about the population, including age, sex, occupation, and location. This information is used by the government to allocate resources effectively and efficiently. For example, if the census data shows a high number of school-age children in a particular area, the government can allocate more resources to education in that area.

subject: Economics
q: An accurate population census is important in order to
explanation: Population census data is needed to guide the planning and development processes in a country. The importance of census as a specific type of accurate statistics of the population characteristics and social trends is the basis for the successful development of a country.This is important as the per-capita income is arrived at by dividing the national income with the population

subject: Economics
q: The major determinant of a floating exchange rate is
explanation: A floating exchange rate is a regime where the currency price of a nation is set by the forex market based on supply and demand relative to other currencies. This is in contrast to a fixed exchange rate, in which the government entirely or predominantly determines the rate.

subject: Economics
q: If the importation of a commodity is limited to a definite quantity, the trade control measure imposed is
explanation: quotas; a limited quantity of a particular product which under official controls can be produced, exported, or imported.

subject: Economics
q: The main objective of WTO is to
options: Assist countries with chronic balance of payments problem
Assist developing countries to execute developmental project
explanation: The main objective of the World Trade Organization (WTO) is to provide a mechanism for tariff reduction. The WTO is an international organization that deals with the global rules of trade between nations. Its main function is to ensure that trade flows as smoothly, predictably, and freely as possible. This is achieved by providing a mechanism for tariff reduction, which helps to eliminate or reduce trade barriers, such as tariffs, and to facilitate open trade.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ECONOMIC ORGANISATION : IBRD, AFDB, UNCTAD ETC

subject: Economics
q: The major determinant of the total volume of output in an economy is the
explanation: The major determinant of the total volume of output in an economy is the size of the labour force. The size of the labour force determines the amount of goods and services that can be produced. If the labour force is large, more goods and services can be produced, leading to a higher total volume of output. The other options, such as the composition of consumer spending, the number of farmers, and the level of total expenditure, can influence the type of goods and services produced, but they do not directly determine the total volume of output.

topics.title: THEORY OF PRODUCTION (2) : CONCEPTS OF PRODUCTION

subject: Economics
q: The labour force of the a country is determined by the
explanation: The labour force of a country is determined by the number of people available for work. This includes those who are currently employed and those who are unemployed but seeking work. The geographical distribution, sex distribution, and age structure of the population can influence the size and composition of the labour force, but they do not directly determine it.

topics.title: LABOUR MARKET AND UNEMPLOYMENT : SUPPLY OF LABOUR

explanation: High dependency ratio is often associated with a country having large percentage of its population as aged and youths. This will tend to aggravate poverty in the economy.

explanation: The Use of Crude implements is often related to agricultural sector in Nigeria and is a major impediment to agricultural productivity.

subject: Economics
q: Cooperative societies are characterized with
explanation: Cooperative societies are characterized by limited liability. This means that the financial liability of the members of the cooperative is limited to the amount they have invested in the cooperative. This is a key characteristic of cooperative societies because it protects the personal assets of the members from being used to pay off the debts of the cooperative. This encourages people to join cooperatives because they know that their personal assets are not at risk.

subject: Economics
q: The marginal propensity to save measures the
options: ratio of change in saving to change in income.
change of supply as a result of a change in consumption.
ratio of change in consumption to a change in the level of income
explanation: The marginal propensity to save (MPS) is an economic concept that shows the proportion of an aggregate raise in pay that a consumer spends on saving rather than on the consumption of goods and services. It is calculated as the change in saving divided by the change in income. Therefore, the correct answer is the ratio of change in saving to change in income.

topics.title: EQUILIBRIUM LEVEL OF NATIONAL INCOME : SAVINGS FUNCTION

explanation: Structural Unemployment is often associated with changes in consumption or demand patterns in an economy leading to changes in industrial structures.

explanation: High cost of conducting census is the major reasons why census was abandoned which is a major problem to Nigeria.

explanation: When government reduces expenditure (fiscal policy) it has the effect of causing prices to fall as demand will be low.

explanation: Transfer earning is concerned with factor earning paid from unemployment benefits. It is mostly paid to pensioners.

explanation: Excess supply or surplus is represented by FG and it is the point above the equilibrium. It occurs when supply is greater than demand leading to fall in price.

subject: Economics
q: The demand for money is referred to as the
options: desire to hold money in tangible assets
desire to hold money in liquid form rather than investing it.
explanation: Demand for money is willingness to hold money in cash than investing it. Lord keynes called demand for money liqudity preference which simply means the desire to hold cash

subject: Economics
q: In the theory of production, a producer is at equilibrium at a point where
options: isocost is tangent to the marginal revenue
isoquant is tangent to the isocost curve.
explanation: In the theory of production, a producer is at equilibrium at a point where the isoquant is tangent to the isocost curve. This is because at this point, the producer is producing at the lowest possible cost. The isoquant curve represents all combinations of inputs that yield the same level of output, while the isocost curve represents all combinations of inputs that cost the same amount. When these two curves are tangent, it means that the producer is using the inputs in the most cost-effective way.

subject: Economics
q: Balance of trade shows the relationship between the
options: total value of visible import and export
total value of invisible import and export
explanation: The balance of trade shows the relationship between the total value of visible import and export. It is the difference between a country's imports and its exports. Balance of trade is the largest component of a country's balance of payments. Deficit and surplus balance of payments is a broader concept that includes not only trade of goods and services, but also financial transfers. Therefore, the correct answer is the total value of visible import and export.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL TRADE : BALANCE OF TRADE & PAYMENT

subject: Economics
q: If the cash reserve ratio is 30%, a new deposit of N20 million will increase money supply by
explanation: The money multiplier is the amount of money that banks generate with each dollar of reserves. The formula for the money multiplier is 1 / Reserve Requirement. In this case, the reserve requirement is 30% or 0.3. So, the money multiplier is 1 / 0.3 = 3.33. Therefore, a new deposit of N20 million will increase the money supply by 3.33 * N20 million = N66.6 million. The closest option to this is N66.7m.

subject: Economics
q: A fall in the price of ostentatious goods will
explanation: A fall in the price of ostentatious goods or veblen goods, will likely decreases demand, as their appeal as status symbols and exclusive items diminishes when they become more accessible.

subject: Economics
q: Payment of interests on loans and the repayment of capital sum at a future date is
explanation:
Debt servicing relates to the payment of initial capital and interest on loan borrowed.

Debt service is the amount of money required over a period of time to repay debts. It includes repayment of: principal and interest. and lease payments (on business vehicles, for example)

subject: Economics
q: The most difficult measure of central tendency to determine in a grouped data is
explanation: The mean is the most difficult measure of central tendency to determine in a grouped data because it requires the sum of all the data points and the total number of data points. The mean is calculated by adding up all the numbers and then dividing by the number of numbers. The other measures, range and variance, are easier to calculate as they do not require the sum of all data points.

explanation: Access to raw materials is crucial in determining the localization of industries e.g In cement manufacturing.

explanation:
Land used for the purpose of agriculture will affect the geographical distribution of population as some states are known to be more fertile in agriculture than others.

Geographic Factors of Population Distribution. Physical conditions, such as climate (temperature and rainfall), landforms in terms of altitudes, the quality of the soil and the availability of energy and mine resources are the important geographical determinants of population distribution.

subject: Economics
q: The tax burden on a commodity will be shared equally if the demand is
explanation: The tax burden on a commodity will be shared equally if the demand is unitary elastic. Elasticity of demand measures how much the quantity demanded changes with a change in price. If the demand is unitary elastic, it means that the percentage change in quantity demanded is exactly equal to the percentage change in price. Therefore, if a tax is imposed on a commodity, the burden of the tax will be shared equally between the consumers and the producers.

subject: Economics
q: Higher income taxes can be used to control
explanation: Demand-pull inflation is often associated with increase in aggregates demand without corresponding increase in supply. Hence, taxes can be imposed on goods to control/ cushion the effect of demand-pull inflation.

explanation: A shift from SoSo to S1S1, implies an increase in quantity supplied. This resulting effect will be a decline in price and increase in quantity.

subject: Economics
q: An indifference map is made up of a set of
explanation: An indifference map is made up of a set of indifference curves. An indifference curve is a graph showing combination of goods that gives the consumer equal satisfaction and utility. Each point on an indifference curve indicates that a consumer is indifferent between the two and all points give him the same utility.

explanation: A policy of recapitalization will help to promote a strong capital base for commercial banks hence reducing the number of liquidating banks.

explanation: A private limited company is a type of business entity in which ownership is shared by a small group of people. According to the Companies Act, the minimum number of members to start a private limited company is two and the maximum number is fifty. Therefore, the correct answer is 'two to fifty'.

subject: Economics
q: The major function of international monetary fund is the
options: provision of short-run loans to medium scale industries
explanation: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) is an international organization that provides financial assistance and advice to member countries. Its primary purpose is to ensure the stability of the international monetary system—the system of exchange rates and international payments that enables countries (and their citizens) to transact with each other. Therefore, the correct answer is 'maintenance of stable exchange rates'.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL ECONOMIC ORGANISATION : OPEC, ECA, IMF

subject: Economics
q: One of the major activities in the upstream sector of the Nigeria petroleum industry includes
explanation: The upstream sector of the petroleum industry refers to the searching for potential underground or underwater crude oil and natural gas fields, drilling of exploratory wells, and subsequently drilling and operating the wells that recover and bring the crude oil or raw natural gas to the surface. Therefore, the correct answer is 'exploration of crude oil'.

topics.title: PETROLEUM AND THE NIGERIA ECONOMY : ONSHORE & OFFSHORE OF THE OIL SECTOR

subject: Economics
q: The law of supply states that the
options: higher the price the higher the quantity supplied
quantity supplied is always equal to the quantity demanded
lower the price the higher the quantity supplied
higher the price the lower the quantity supplied
explanation: The law of supply is a fundamental principle in economics that states that, assuming all else being equal, an increase in the price of goods will have a direct relationship with the quantity of the goods produced by producers. In other words, the higher the price, the higher the quantity supplied. Producers supply more at a higher price because selling a higher quantity at a higher price increases revenue.

topics.title: THEORY OF SUPPLY : MEANING AND DETERMINANTS

subject: Economics
q: One of the features of a perfect competitive firm is
explanation: In perfect market, there is no transport cost due to the mobility of factors of production and free entry and exit.

subject: Economics
q: The basis for international trade is the
explanation: The basis for international trade is the difference in natural resources endowment. Countries trade with each other when they have a surplus of the resources that are in demand by other countries. Natural resources endowment refers to the naturally occurring assets including mineral deposits, soil fertility, climatic conditions favorable for agriculture, and other factors. Countries with abundant natural resources export them to countries with less. This forms the basis for international trade.

topics.title: INTERNATIONAL TRADE : PRINCIPLES OF INTERNATIONAL TRADE

subject: Economics
q: Training farmers on the improved farming techniques is the major activity of
explanation: Extension workers are professionals who work in the agricultural sector to provide training, resources, and advice to farmers. They play a crucial role in educating farmers about improved farming techniques, new technologies, and sustainable farming practices. Therefore, training farmers on improved farming techniques is a major activity of extension workers.

subject: Economics
q: In a free market economy, prices are determined by
explanation: In a free market economy, prices are determined by the forces of demand and supply. This is because a free market economy is an economic system where decisions regarding investment, production, and distribution are guided by the price signals created by the forces of supply and demand. The central government does not interfere in setting prices, and neither do consumers or producers individually. They all respond to the price signals in the market.

topics.title: THE THEORY OF PRICE DETERMINATION : DETERMINANTS OF PRICE UNDER FREE MARKET

explanation: What to produce is mostly affected by the availability of resources in the economy as no producer or economy can think of producing without first counting the cost.

explanation: A fall in petrol supply, will mean no hoarding and black marketing which will help to reduce the price of goods in the market generally.

subject: Economics
q: The short-run equilibrium point of a perfectly competitive firm is attained at a point where
options: the demand curve is tangential to the marginal cost
the marginal cost curve is equal to the marginal revenue curve
the marginal cost is equal to the price of the sum
explanation: In the short-run equilibrium of a perfectly competitive firm, the firm's marginal cost (MC) is equal to its marginal revenue (MR). This is because, in perfect competition, firms are price takers and can sell as much output as they want at the market price. Therefore, they will produce up to the point where the cost of producing an additional unit (MC) is equal to the revenue gained from selling that additional unit (MR).

explanation: By maximum price, the commodities are sold below the equilibrium price and there will be excess demand over supply

q: If a fall in the price of commodity K brought about an increase in the demand for commodity Y, the two commodities are
explanation: Complementary demand is generated for a product as a result of demand for a related but different product e.g computers and software, vehicles and tyres etc. It is also known as joint demand.

topics.title: THE THEORY OF DEMAND : TYPES OF DEMAND

subject: Economics
q: In the theory of production, the price of a factor inputs is determined by
explanation: In the theory of production, the price of a factor inputs is determined by its elasticity of demand. Elasticity of demand refers to the degree of responsiveness of quantity demanded to a change in the price of the factor input. If the demand for a factor input is elastic, a small change in price will result in a large change in quantity demanded. Conversely, if the demand for a factor input is inelastic, a change in price will result in a small change in quantity demanded.

explanation: In economics, when a monopolist's selling price is below his short-run marginal cost, it means that the cost of producing an additional unit of output is more than the price at which it is sold. This implies that the monopolist is making losses. Therefore, the correct answer is 'losses'.

subject: Economics
q: The overall economic performance of a country can be assessed through
explanation: The overall economic performance of a country is best assessed through its national income estimates. National income estimates provide a comprehensive measure of a nation's overall economic activity. It includes the total income earned by a nation's people, including wages, rents, interest, and profits. It is a broad measure that reflects the total economic output of a country, and thus, it is a good indicator of economic performance.

subject: Economics
q: The supply of agricultural products is mainly determined by
explanation: The supply of agricultural products is mainly determined by technology. Technology in agriculture involves the use of machinery and other advanced techniques to increase the efficiency and output of agricultural processes. It does not depend on consumer's taste, income or consumers themselves as these factors are more related to the demand side of the market.

subject: Economics
q: The failure of the Nigeria industrial sector is mainly associated with
explanation: The failure of the Nigeria industrial sector is mainly associated with underdeveloped infrastructural facilities. Infrastructure is the backbone of any country's economic development. It is an umbrella term that includes physical structures, systems, and facilities needed for the operation of a society or enterprise. In the context of an industrial sector, infrastructure refers to facilities and systems that support manufacturing and production, such as transportation, electricity, water supply, and telecommunications. Without well-developed infrastructure, industries cannot operate efficiently, leading to their failure.

explanation: The long-run total cost curve shows the cost of all factor inputs that are employed. In the long run, all factors of production are variable, and firms adjust all types of inputs to achieve the lowest total cost. Therefore, the long-run total cost curve represents the cost of all factor inputs that are employed.

q: Import substitution policy is aimed at
options: setting up local industries to produce goods formerly imported
banning the importation of goods locally produced
explanation:
Import substitution is a policy of industrialization by the government aimed at encouraging the local manufacturers to produce formerly imported goods.
Import substitution industrialization (ISI) is a trade and economic policy which advocates replacing foreign imports with domestic production. ISI is based on the premise that a country should attempt to reduce its foreign dependency through the local production of industrialized products.

explanation: In the short-run equilibrium of a perfectly competitive firm, the firm's marginal cost (MC) is equal to its marginal revenue (MR). This is because, in perfect competition, firms are price takers and can sell as much as they want at the market price. Therefore, they will produce up to the point where the cost of producing an additional unit (MC) is equal to the revenue gained from selling that additional unit (MR).

subject: Economics
q: A country seeking loan for the correction of balance of payments problems will approach
explanation: International Monetary Fund assist countries to correct Balance of payment deficits by giving them short term loans.

subject: Economics
q: One of the major factors militating against industrialization in Nigeria is
explanation: Industrialization is the process by which an economy is transformed from primarily agricultural to one based on the manufacturing of goods. This process requires a significant amount of infrastructure, such as roads, ports, power plants, and other facilities. In Nigeria, there is a significant lack of these infrastructural facilities, which hinders the process of industrialization. Therefore, the correct answer is 'inadequacy of infrastructural facilities'.

q: In order to add value to Nigeria agricultural produce, there is need to
explanation: Adding value to agricultural produce often involves processing them into finished goods. This is because processing increases the usefulness of the product, enhances its aesthetic appeal, and makes it more marketable and profitable. Advertising them in European markets, adopting modern storage methods, and cultivating high breed crops are all important, but they do not directly add value to the produce in the same way that processing does.

explanation: Life assurance basically keep peoples' asset in the form of long-term financial instrument since it has to do with life and the next of kin can always acquire such in the long term.
